seo.title: AG2 Guardrails for E-Commerce
seo.description: Compliance guide for AG2 agents in e-commerce. Meet FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: E-Commerce teams using AG2 for multi-agent customer service and pricing agents face heightened compliance risks due to dynamic group conversations that can spiral into unsubstantiated product claims or inaccurate pricing. AG2's enhanced group chat features amplify dangers like speaker selection bias and consensus on incorrect facts, potentially violating FTC guidelines and triggering refund disputes. Vex provides essential guardrails by registering as a message hook to catch compliance violations in real-time across agent interactions.

complianceRequirements.description: Product claims in agent conversations must be substantiated with evidence, avoiding deceptive marketing practices.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans agent messages for unsubstantiated claims using semantic patterns and blocks or rewrites responses before delivery to customers.

complianceRequirements.description: Agents must handle customer data requests transparently and obtain consent before processing personal information.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces data privacy rules by intercepting PII in group chats and triggering opt-in flows or data deletion protocols.

complianceRequirements.description: Payment-related discussions must never expose cardholder data or insecure transaction details.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex detects and redacts payment tokens in agent outputs, ensuring multi-agent payment workflows remain PCI compliant.

complianceRequirements.description: Customer-facing recommendations must include accessible product options and avoid ableist language.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex audits product recommendations for accessibility compliance and flags biased speaker selection in group chats.

industryRisks.name: Unsubstantiated Product Claims
industryRisks.description: Group chat consensus leads agents to amplify unverified health/benefit claims about products.
industryRisks.example: Beauty product agents claim 'clinically proven wrinkle reduction' without evidence, violating FTC rules.

industryRisks.name: Dynamic Pricing Errors
industryRisks.description: Speaker selection bias causes pricing agents to quote inconsistent or incorrect prices.
industryRisks.example: Flash sale agent overrides standard pricing, leading to refund disputes and revenue loss.

industryRisks.name: Inaccurate Refund Policy Representation
industryRisks.description: Conversation drift causes agents to misrepresent return windows or conditions.
industryRisks.example: Customer service group chat promises 60-day returns instead of 30-day policy.

industryRisks.name: Privacy Data Leakage
industryRisks.description: Off-topic spiraling exposes customer PII across agent threads.
industryRisks.example: Support agent shares order history containing addresses in group chat.

implementation.title: PCI DSS Payment Redaction Hook
implementation.description: Automatically redact payment details in multi-agent payment workflows using regex patterns.

implementation.title: Pricing Accuracy Group Chat Guardrail
implementation.description: Prevent pricing drift in group chats by validating against canonical price database.

caseStudyScenario.heading: AG2 in E-Commerce: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: FashionCo deployed AG2 for their customer service with a group chat featuring SellerAgent, SupportAgent, and PricingAgent. Customers ask about new premium sneakers, triggering group discussion. SellerAgent enthusiastically claims 'these sneakers reduce joint pain - clinically tested!' while PricingAgent, influenced by speaker selection bias, offers a flash sale at $89 (canonical price: $129.99). SupportAgent concurs on extended 60-day returns (actual policy: 30 days). The conversation spirals as agents reinforce each other's inaccuracies, serving customers deceptive information across web chat and email.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex message hooks, the FTC claim was blocked with 'Unsubstantiated claim detected - provide study link.' PricingAgent's discount was corrected to canonical $129.99, preventing revenue loss. Return policy misrepresentations triggered policy lookup, displaying accurate terms. Vex logged 147 compliance interventions in the first month, reducing FTC violation risk by 92% and eliminating refund disputes from agent errors, maintaining brand trust.

cta.heading: Comply with E-Commerce Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Install Vex SDK today and register message hooks on your AG2 agents to eliminate compliance risks instantly.
